description: Calisthenics progression training app — fork workout-cool and transform it into a progression-focused calisthenics platform with exercise families, level tracking, assessments, and program templates.

userStories.title: Strip gym-specific pages and tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove gym-specific pages (BMI calculator, calorie calculator, heart-rate zones) and ads components from the forked workout-cool codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove BMI calculator page and all related components/logic under tools/bmi-calculator
Remove calorie calculator page and all related components/logic under tools/calorie-calculator
Remove heart-rate zones page and all related components/logic under tools/heart-rate-zones
Remove ads components from src/components/ads
Remove license system models, routes, and UI
Remove any navigation links or references to removed pages
App builds without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Strip workout builder feature
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove the 3-step workout builder (equipment → muscles → generate) since it will be replaced by calisthenics program templates.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the workout-builder feature directory (src/features/workout-builder)
Remove any pages/routes that use the workout builder
Remove navigation links to the workout builder
Keep the workout-session feature (logging/tracking) intact
App builds without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove EAV exercise attribute system from Prisma schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove the existing EAV (Entity-Attribute-Value) exercise attribute models to make room for the new rigid calisthenics schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove ExerciseAttribute, ExerciseAttributeName, ExerciseAttributeValue models from prisma/schema.prisma
Remove ExerciseAttributeNameEnum and ExerciseAttributeValueEnum enums from prisma/schema.prisma
Remove any relations to these models from the Exercise model
Remove the existing sample-exercises.csv seed data and import scripts that use the old attribute system
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This will temporarily break exercise-related features until the new schema is in place. That is expected.

userStories.title: Add calisthenics enums to Prisma schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the enum types that the new calisthenics exercise schema will use.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ExerciseCategory enum: push_horizontal, push_vertical, pull_horizontal, pull_vertical, core_anterior, core_posterior, core_stability, legs, skills, mobility
Add DifficultyLevel enum: beginner, intermediate, advanced, elite
Add MeasurementType enum: reps, time, reps_each_side, time_each_side

Add MuscleGroup enum with values: chest, anterior_deltoid, triceps, serratus_anterior, latissimus_dorsi, rhomboids, rear_deltoid, biceps, brachialis, forearms, rectus_abdominis, obliques, transverse_abdominis, hip_flexors, erector_spinae, quadratus_lumborum, quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, calves, hip_adductors, hip_abductors
Add EquipmentType enum: bar, parallels, rings, band, wall, floor, box, bench
Add EquipmentHeight enum: low, medium, high
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add new Exercise model to Prisma schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the new Exercise model with calisthenics-specific fields replacing the old EAV approach.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify Exercise model to include: name_en (String), name_fr (String), slug (String, unique), description (String), instructions (String[]), cues (String[]), commonMistakes (String[]), videoUrl (String, optional), imageUrls (String[]), difficultyLevel (DifficultyLevel enum), measurementType (MeasurementType enum), defaultSets (Int), defaultReps (Int, optional), defaultHoldTime (Int, optional), restBetweenSets (Int), category (ExerciseCategory enum), subcategory (String), primaryMuscles (MuscleGroup[]), secondaryMuscles (MuscleGroup[]), bandAssistance (Boolean, default false), bandResistance (Boolean, default false)
Keep existing relations to WorkoutSessionExercise and UserFavoriteExercise
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Equipment and ExerciseEquipment models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Equipment and ExerciseEquipment models to track what equipment each exercise requires.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Equipment model: id (String, cuid), name (String), type (EquipmentType enum), heightVariant (EquipmentHeight, optional), portable (Boolean, default false)
Add ExerciseEquipment join model linking Exercise to Equipment (many-to-many)
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add ProgressionFamily and ProgressionLevel models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need models to represent progression families (e.g., Push-Up Family) and their ordered levels linking to exercises.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ProgressionFamily model: id (String, cuid), name (String), slug (String, unique), description (String), category (ExerciseCategory enum)

Add ProgressionLevel model: id (String, cuid), familyId (relation to ProgressionFamily), exerciseId (relation to Exercise), level (Int), unlockCriteria (Json — schema: { minReps, minSets, minHoldTime, minFormQuality, consistencyWeeks })
Add unique constraint on (familyId, level) to prevent duplicate levels
Add unique constraint on (familyId, exerciseId) to prevent duplicate exercise assignments
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add UserProgression and UserEquipment models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need models to track each user's current progression level per family and their available equipment.
acceptanceCriteria: Add UserProgression model: id (String, cuid), userId (relation to User), progressionFamilyId (relation to ProgressionFamily), currentLevel (Int, default 1), unlockedAt (DateTime, default now), lastAssessedAt (DateTime, optional)
Add unique constraint on (userId, progressionFamilyId)
Add UserEquipment join model: userId (relation to User), equipmentId (relation to Equipment)
Add unique constraint on (userId, equipmentId)
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Assessment model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Assessment model to record level-up test attempts and results.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Assessment model: id (String, cuid), userId (relation to User), progressionFamilyId (relation to ProgressionFamily), exerciseTestedId (relation to Exercise), date (DateTime, default now), repsCompleted (Int, optional), holdTimeSeconds (Int, optional), formQuality (FormQuality enum: poor/acceptable/good/excellent), rpe (Int, optional), passed (Boolean)
Add FormQuality enum if not already present
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Modify WorkoutSet model for calisthenics
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to adapt the WorkoutSet model to track calisthenics-specific data instead of gym weight data.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove weight-related fields from WorkoutSet (types, intValues, secondValues, units arrays or equivalent weight tracking)
Add holdTimeSeconds (Int, optional) to WorkoutSet
Add formQuality (FormQuality enum, optional) to WorkoutSet
Add BandLevel enum: none, light, medium, heavy, extra_heavy
Add bandUsed (BandLevel, optional, default none) to WorkoutSet
Add rpe (Int, optional) to WorkoutSet
Keep reps field (Int, optional) on WorkoutSet
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema complete per audit 2026-03-05. WorkoutSet now has holdTimeSeconds, formQuality (FormQuality enum), bandUsed (BandLevel enum), rpe fields. Weight fields removed. UI components in src/features/workout-session/ui/ still need verification — confirm weight inputs are removed and calisthenics inputs are shown.

userStories.title: Seed equipment data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to seed the equipment table with standard calisthenics equipment.
acceptanceCriteria: Create seed script at scripts/seed-equipment.ts (or add to existing seed file)
Seed 8+ equipment items: pull-up bar (high), parallel bars low, parallel bars medium, parallel bars high, gymnastic rings, resistance bands, wall, floor, box/bench
Seed is idempotent (uses upsert or checks for existing records)
Seed runs successfully via npx prisma db seed or a script command
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. First seed to run — unblocks all exercise and progression features. Use upsert for idempotency.

userStories.title: Seed push-up and pull-up progression families with exercises
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to seed the first two progression families (Push-Up and Pull-Up) with their exercises and levels.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed Push-Up Family with 10 levels: Wall Push-Ups (L1), Incline Push-Ups High (L2), Incline Push-Ups Low (L3), Knee Push-Ups (L4), Full Push-Ups (L5), Diamond Push-Ups (L6), Archer Push-Ups (L7), Pseudo Planche Push-Ups (L8), One-Arm Push-Up Assisted (L9), One-Arm Push-Up (L10)

Seed Pull-Up Family with 10 levels: Dead Hang (L1), Scapular Pulls (L2), Negative Pull-Ups (L3), Band-Assisted Pull-Ups (L4), Full Pull-Ups (L5), Wide/Close/Chin-Ups (L6), L-Sit Pull-Ups (L7), Weighted Pull-Ups (L8), Archer Pull-Ups (L9), One-Arm Pull-Up (L10)
Each exercise has: name (en/fr), slug, description, instructions, cues, commonMistakes, category, muscles, difficulty, measurementType, defaults, equipment links
Each ProgressionLevel has unlock criteria JSON (minReps, minSets, minHoldTime, minFormQuality as applicable)
Seed is idempotent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. MVP simplification: minimum viable content per exercise (name en/fr, slug, description, instructions[], cues[], category, muscles, difficulty, measurementType, defaultSets, defaultReps/HoldTime). Full content (commonMistakes, videoUrl, imageUrls) can be enriched post-launch.

userStories.title: Seed dip and squat progression families with exercises
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to seed the Dip and Squat progression families with their exercises and levels.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed Dip Family with 8-10 levels from bench dips through ring dips
Seed Squat Family with 8-10 levels from assisted squats through pistol squats
Each exercise has full content (name en/fr, slug, description, instructions, cues, commonMistakes, category, muscles, difficulty, measurementType, defaults, equipment links)
Each ProgressionLevel has unlock criteria JSON
Seed is idempotent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Same MVP simplification as US-012 — minimum viable content first, enrich post-launch.

userStories.title: Seed L-sit and front lever progression families with exercises
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to seed the L-Sit and Front Lever progression families with their exercises and levels.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed L-Sit Family with 8-10 levels from tucked hold through V-sit
Seed Front Lever Family with 10 levels from dead hang through front lever pull-ups
Each exercise has full content (name en/fr, slug, description, instructions, cues, commonMistakes, category, muscles, difficulty, measurementType, defaults, equipment links)
Each ProgressionLevel has unlock criteria JSON
Seed is idempotent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Same MVP simplification as US-012 — minimum viable content first, enrich post-launch.

userStories.title: Seed remaining standalone exercises
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to seed additional calisthenics exercises that are not part of the 6 progression families but are used in programs and general training.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed 40-60 additional exercises covering: core work (planks, hollow body, dragon flags), mobility (shoulder dislocates, pike stretches, bridges), warm-up movements, supplementary push/pull/leg exercises
Each exercise has full content fields populated
Equipment relationships set correctly
Seed is idempotent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. The 6 progression families (US-012 to US-014) cover all critical exercises for MVP. Standalone core/mobility/supplementary exercises can be added post-launch once the app is live and feedback is gathered.

userStories.title: Add user experience level and goals to User model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to extend the User model with calisthenics-specific profile fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ExperienceLevel enum: complete_beginner, some_experience, intermediate, advanced
Add experienceLevel (ExperienceLevel, optional) to User model
Add trainingGoals (String[], default []) to User model for storing goal identifiers like 'first_pull_up', 'muscle_up', 'front_lever', 'general_fitness', 'pistol_squat', 'handstand'
Generate and run migration successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Requires schema migration. Onboarding customization (US-022, US-023) deferred with it. For v0.1, all users start at progression level 1 and test up.

userStories.title: Create progression server actions and queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need server actions to query and update user progression data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/features/progression-system/ directory following Feature-Sliced Design
Implement getUserProgressions(userId) — returns all progression family statuses with current level, exercise name, next level info
Implement getProgressionFamily(familySlug) — returns family with all levels and exercises
Implement advanceUserLevel(userId, familyId) — validates unlock criteria met and advances level
Implement initializeUserProgressions(userId) — creates level 1 entries for all families (called on account creation)
All actions use Prisma and are server-side
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Create src/features/progression-system/actions/. For v0.1: getUserProgressions and getProgressionFamily are must-have. advanceUserLevel and initializeUserProgressions can be stubs that are filled in when assessment UI ships in v0.2.

userStories.title: Create equipment server actions and queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need server actions to manage user equipment selections.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/features/equipment-setup/ directory following Feature-Sliced Design
Implement getAllEquipment() — returns all available equipment items
Implement getUserEquipment(userId) — returns user's selected equipment
Implement updateUserEquipment(userId, equipmentIds[]) — replaces user's equipment selection
All actions use Prisma and are server-side
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. getAllEquipment and getUserEquipment needed for v0.1 (exercise filtering). updateUserEquipment needed when equipment selection UI ships — can be a stub for now.

userStories.title: Create exercise server actions and queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need server actions to query exercises with the new schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Update or create src/entities/exercise/ to work with new Exercise model
Implement getExercises(filters) — returns exercises filtered by category, muscle, equipment, difficulty, with search by name
Implement getExerciseBySlug(slug) — returns full exercise details including progression family/level info
Implement getExercisesForUserEquipment(userId) — returns only exercises matching user's equipment
All actions use Prisma and are server-side
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. getExercises(filters) and getExerciseBySlug(slug) are must-have for browse and detail pages. getExercisesForUserEquipment can be a lightweight wrapper built on top of getExercises.

userStories.title: Create assessment server actions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need server actions to handle level-up assessment attempts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/features/assessment/ directory following Feature-Sliced Design
Implement submitAssessment(userId, familyId, exerciseId, { reps, holdTime, formQuality, rpe }) — evaluates against unlock criteria, saves result, advances level if passed
Implement getAssessmentHistory(userId, familyId) — returns past assessment attempts for a family
Assessment evaluation logic compares submitted values against ProgressionLevel.unlockCriteria JSON
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Assessment/level-up system requires both submitAssessment logic and UI (US-029). Progression dashboard (US-027/028) ships in v0.1 with read-only views and a placeholder 'Test My Level' button.

userStories.title: Build equipment selection UI component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a multi-select UI for choosing my available equipment with icons.
acceptanceCriteria: Create equipment selector component in src/features/equipment-setup/ui/
Shows all equipment items as selectable cards/chips with icons (use lucide-react icons)
Multi-select: user can toggle each equipment item on/off
Calls updateUserEquipment server action on save
Uses existing shadcn/ui and DaisyUI components for styling
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Equipment UI selector depends on US-018 (equipment actions). For v0.1 all exercises are shown; users can filter manually on the exercise browse page.

userStories.title: Add equipment selection to onboarding flow
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to select my equipment during onboarding so the app is personalized from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Add equipment selection step to existing onboarding flow (after account creation)
Reuses the equipment selector component from US-021
Equipment selection is saved to database via server action
User can proceed without selecting any equipment (defaults to floor + wall)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Current onboarding page is a stub — for v0.1 leave it as-is or add a minimal 'get started' CTA. Full onboarding with equipment selection comes in v0.2 with US-021.

userStories.title: Add experience level and goals to onboarding flow
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to set my experience level and training goals during onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: Add experience level step to onboarding: radio/card select with options complete_beginner, some_experience, intermediate, advanced
Add goals step: multi-select cards for goals (first pull-up, muscle-up, front lever, general fitness, pistol squat, handstand)
Save experience level and goals to user model via server action
Initialize user progressions after onboarding completes (call initializeUserProgressions)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Depends on US-016 (User model extension with experienceLevel/trainingGoals) which is also deferred.

userStories.title: Add equipment selection to profile/settings page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to update my equipment from my profile settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add equipment section to existing profile/settings page
Reuses the equipment selector component from US-021
Shows currently selected equipment on load
Save button updates equipment via server action
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Ships with US-021 equipment selector component.

userStories.title: Build exercise browse/search page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse and search the exercise library with filters.
acceptanceCriteria: Create exercise list page at /[locale]/(app)/exercises
Filter dropdowns for: category, primary muscle, equipment, difficulty level
Text search input for exercise name
Filters persist in URL params (use nuqs)
Toggle to show only exercises matching user's equipment
Exercise cards show: name, category badge, difficulty badge, primary muscles
Clicking a card navigates to /exercises/[slug]
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Depends on US-019 (exercise server actions). Use nuqs for URL params. For v0.1, equipment toggle filter can be simplified to a category/muscle/difficulty filter only — equipment-based filtering can come with US-021 in v0.2.

userStories.title: Build exercise detail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view full details of an exercise including video, instructions, and progression context.
acceptanceCriteria: Create exercise detail page at /[locale]/(app)/exercises/[slug]

Displays: name, YouTube video embed (if videoUrl exists), description, step-by-step instructions list, form cues list, common mistakes list, category, muscles worked (primary + secondary), difficulty badge, required equipment
If exercise belongs to a progression family, shows: family name, current level, previous/next exercise links
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Depends on US-019 (getExerciseBySlug). Progression context (previous/next exercise in family) requires join through ProgressionLevel table.

userStories.title: Build progression dashboard page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a page showing my current level in each progression family.
acceptanceCriteria: Create progression page at /[locale]/(app)/progression
Shows 6 progression family cards in a grid
Each card displays: family name, current level number, current exercise name, next exercise name, progress bar (level X/10)
Each card shows unlock criteria for next level
Clicking a card navigates to /progression/[familySlug]
Add navigation link to progression page in app sidebar/nav
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Depends on US-017 (getUserProgressions). For v0.1: show all 6 family cards with current level and next exercise. 'Test My Level' button can appear but link to a 'Coming soon' state since assessment UI (US-029) is deferred.

userStories.title: Build progression family detail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all levels in a progression family with my current position.
acceptanceCriteria: Create detail page at /[locale]/(app)/progression/[familySlug]
Shows all levels as a vertical list/timeline
Unlocked levels (at or below current) visually distinct (e.g., colored, checkmark)
Current level highlighted prominently
Locked levels shown greyed with unlock criteria text
Each level shows exercise name and links to exercise detail page
Includes 'Test My Level' button for the next locked level
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Depends on US-017 (getProgressionFamily). Shows all levels as vertical timeline with locked/unlocked states. 'Test My Level' button disabled/placeholder for v0.1 — full assessment flow is US-029 in v0.2.

userStories.title: Build assessment/level-up flow UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to take a level-up test from the progression detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Test My Level' button opens assessment modal/page
Shows the target exercise name and unlock criteria (e.g., '3 sets of 8 reps with good form')
Form inputs for: reps completed (if rep-based), hold time in seconds (if time-based), form quality selector (poor/acceptable/good/excellent), RPE slider (1-10)
Submit button calls submitAssessment server action
On pass: success message with celebration (can use canvas-confetti already in deps), level updates in UI
On fail: encouraging message showing what criteria were not met
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Requires US-020 (assessment server actions). Progression detail page (US-028) ships with a placeholder 'Test My Level' button in v0.1.

userStories.title: Update workout set logging UI for calisthenics
userStories.description: As a user, I want the set logging UI to show calisthenics-appropriate inputs instead of weight inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Update workout session exercise card to remove weight/unit inputs
For rep-based exercises: show reps input field
For time-based exercises: show hold time (seconds) input field
Add optional form quality dropdown (poor/acceptable/good/excellent) per set
Add optional band level dropdown (none/light/medium/heavy/extra_heavy) per set
Add optional RPE selector (1-10) per set
Set data saves to modified WorkoutSet model via existing session actions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. The WorkoutSet schema is already correct (US-010). This story is about verifying and updating the UI components. Check src/features/workout-session/ui/ for weight-related inputs and replace with reps/holdTime/formQuality/bandUsed/rpe. UX audit confirmed the fields exist in the UI but need verification.

userStories.title: Seed program templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to seed 4-5 pre-built calisthenics program templates using the existing Program infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed 'Beginner Full Body' program: 3x/week, 4 weeks, uses basic progression exercises, requires floor + pull-up bar
Seed 'Muscle-Up Prep' program: 3x/week, 6 weeks, pull-up and dip focused, requires bar + parallels
Seed 'Front Lever Program' program: 3x/week, 6 weeks, pull-focused, requires bar
Seed 'Minimal Equipment' program: 3x/week, 4 weeks, floor + wall only
Seed 'Park Workout' program: 3x/week, 4 weeks, requires bar + parallels
Each program has: title (en/fr), description, level, equipment requirements, weeks with sessions, sessions with exercises and suggested sets
Seed is idempotent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Program templates require significant seed data effort. For v0.1, users can still use any existing programs in the database. Program templates are the second thing to add after core progression features.

userStories.title: Update program listing to filter by user equipment
userStories.description: As a user, I want the program listing page to show only programs I can do with my equipment.
acceptanceCriteria: Update existing programs listing page to check user's equipment against program requirements
Programs compatible with user's equipment shown prominently
Incompatible programs shown greyed or in a separate 'Requires additional equipment' section with missing equipment listed
If user has no equipment set, show all programs with a prompt to set up equipment
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DEFERRED to v0.2. Depends on US-021 (equipment selector) and US-031 (program templates). For v0.1 all programs shown; users can manually pick what fits their equipment.

userStories.title: Build home dashboard with stats and progression summary
userStories.description: As a user, I want a home dashboard showing my recent activity and progression overview.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update authenticated home page at /[locale]/(app)/ or /dashboard
Stats section: workouts this week count, current streak (consecutive days/weeks with workouts), total workouts all time
Progression summary: compact list/grid showing each family name + current level (e.g., 'Push-Ups: Level 5')
Recent workouts: last 3-5 sessions with date, program name, duration
Links to progression page and exercise library
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Home page is currently a stub ('Dashboard coming soon'). Replace with real dashboard: workout stats (this week, streak, total), progression summary grid (6 families × current level), last 3-5 sessions. Depends on US-017 (getUserProgressions) and existing workout session queries.

userStories.title: Add navigation links for new pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want navigation links to the new calisthenics-specific pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Progression' link to main app navigation/sidebar pointing to /progression
Add 'Exercises' link to main app navigation/sidebar pointing to /exercises
Add 'Dashboard' link if not already the home page
Keep existing navigation items (Programs, Profile, Leaderboard, Statistics)
Remove links to any stripped pages (tools, etc.)
Navigation works on mobile (responsive sidebar/menu)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: v0.1 CRITICAL. Fast win — 15-30 min task. Add 'Progression' and 'Exercises' to BottomNavigation.tsx and sidebar. Remove any dead links to stripped pages. Do this last, after pages exist to link to.
